repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1: named DML/LRO/ADML ATE reports share the common EIF/variance map at truth and collapse in the standard product-rate region

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ + ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML + Newey1994Econometrica: orthogonal/common-EIF guardrail under standard conditions

repo_prior_art.cite: closest is eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1, but it only gives common truth-level variance maps and a certified-rule frontier, not a finite-support pseudo-truth compatibility partition with numerator and variance-report cells

published_prior_art.cite: none; closest checklist papers give DML product-rate validity, LRO/ADML constructions, one-good-nuisance irregularity or Riesz equivalences, not the three-report necessary-and-sufficient finite-support partition

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1 and v2 angle-0 already used finite-support witnesses for report separation; this witness is narrower and tied to non-rectangular compatibility rather than certified projection indices

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyImbensWager2018JRSSB gives overlap/balancing motivation but not a DML/LRO/ADML non-rectangular pseudo-truth witness

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The rate tuple allows r_g, r_a^p, r_a^A = 0, but the partition is written using first-order Gateaux/local drift expansions; non-vanishing inconsistent nuisances need either exact finite-support nonlinear conditions or an added small-amplitude local-path convention.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The setup uses baseline coefficients representing mu_10 and mu_00 in the fixed B-span, but §7 only explicitly assumes baseline Riesz representability; add outcome-regression representability or weaken the setup to approximation errors.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Split the partition into a genuinely local r>0 theorem and a separate exact finite-support pseudo-truth cell calculation for r=0 one-good-nuisance regimes.
Add an explicit assumption that B represents both baseline outcome regressions, or define the residual approximation bias and carry it through R_m and S_m.

For Conjecture 2, name the specific marginal-rate/product-rate workflow being refuted, preferably ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ-style product-rate diagnostics or a calibrated-DML diagnostic rule, not only generic rectangular summaries.

recommended_next_step: Revise the local-vs-inconsistent nuisance convention and add the missing outcome-span assumption before moving this proposal forward.
